# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build with Command Input

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build Job
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      build_status: './build_status'

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Python 3.x
      uses: actions/setup-python@v2
      with:
        python-version: 3.x

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install dependencies

    - name: Run custom build command
      run: |
        ${{ if eq(inputs.customCommand, '') }}echo No custom command provided.${{ else }} ${{ steps.shell_command.outputs.result }}${{ endif }}
      env:
        CUSTOM_COMMAND: ${{ inputs.customCommand }}

    - name: Shell command (for custom build commands)
      id: shell_command
      uses: azure/virtual-env@v1
      with:
        source=python3
        python=3
        pip=pip3
        script: |
          if [[ -n "${CUSTOM_COMMAND}" ]]; then
            echo "Running custom command: ${{ env.CUSTOM_COMMAND }}"
            eval "${CUSTOM_COMMAND}"
            exit $?
          fi
          echo "No custom command provided, running default build process."
